mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market, increasing corporate profit margins through wage growth and productivity gains, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of declining UNRATE are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or lag behind sector-specific employment trends.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in 60%+ of cases or shows significant divergence in sectoral demand patterns.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts (HOUST) signals weak consumer demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors price in economic slowdown risks.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive despite HOUST declines exceeding 5% in 60%+ of cases or show no correlation with housing market dynamics.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive during CPIAUCSL > 3% periods or show no significant correlation with inflationary pressures across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns fail to show significant positive correlation with INDPRO increases or exhibit divergent performance during supply-side shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a robust labor market, which drives consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in 60%+ of cases or shows significant sectoral divergence in employment trends.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain negative despite RSAFS growth exceeding 2% in 60%+ of cases or show no correlation with retail and service sector performance.
